items.briefing:
The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) has warned that login credentials for Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways have been exposed, meaning attackers could potentially access your organisation's network. If your workplace uses Fortinet products, IT staff should check for unauthorised access and update credentials immediately. Separately, a Cloudflare report highlights that cyberattacks on civil society organisations — including Australian ones — are increasing, so smaller organisations should not assume they are too minor to be targeted.

An IBM study also notes that many executives are struggling to manage where their AI data is stored and controlled, which is a growing risk for organisations handling sensitive information. **Practical tip:** If you use a VPN to connect to work, contact your IT team to confirm your login credentials have been reviewed and changed recently.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: ACSC warns of widespread credential exposure affecting critical infrastructure firewalls used across Australian organisations.
